---
title: "Retail Seasonality & Timing Dashboard"
subtitle: "When Do Customers Buy? A Seasonal Analysis of Retail Sales"
author: "Jake Evans"
date: today
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    orientation: columns
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com/jakevns/RStudio
        aria-label: GitHub repository
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(gt)

sales <- read_csv("data/Retail_sales.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  rename(
    store_id         = `Store ID`,
    product_id       = `Product ID`,
    date             = Date,
    units_sold       = `Units Sold`,
    revenue          = `Sales Revenue (USD)`,
    discount_pct     = `Discount Percentage`,
    marketing_spend  = `Marketing Spend (USD)`,
    store_location   = `Store Location`,
    category         = `Product Category`,
    day_of_week      = `Day of the Week`,
    holiday          = `Holiday Effect`
  ) |>
  mutate(
    date        = as_date(date),
    month       = month(date, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE),
    year        = year(date),
    day_of_week = factor(
      day_of_week,
      levels = c("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday",
                 "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday")
    )
  )

gold_green <- c("#1B4332", "#2C5F2D", "#52796F", "#84A98C", "#CAD2C5")
```

```{r}
#| label: calcs

total_revenue <- sum(sales$revenue)

monthly_revenue <- sales |>
  group_by(month) |>
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(revenue), .groups = "drop")

peak_month <- monthly_revenue |>
  slice_max(total_revenue, n = 1) |>
  pull(month) |>
  as.character()

dow_revenue <- sales |>
  group_by(day_of_week) |>
  summarise(avg_revenue = mean(revenue), .groups = "drop")

best_day <- dow_revenue |>
  slice_max(avg_revenue, n = 1) |>
  pull(day_of_week) |>
  as.character()

holiday_compare <- sales |>
  group_by(holiday) |>
  summarise(avg_revenue = mean(revenue), .groups = "drop")

holiday_avg     <- holiday_compare |> filter(holiday == TRUE)  |> pull(avg_revenue)
non_holiday_avg <- holiday_compare |> filter(holiday == FALSE) |> pull(avg_revenue)
holiday_lift    <- (holiday_avg - non_holiday_avg) / non_holiday_avg * 100
```

# Overview

## Column {width="65%"}

### Row {height="25%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Revenue"
#| icon: cash-coin
#| color: primary
list(value = dollar(total_revenue, largest_with_cents = 0, scale = 1/1e6, suffix = "M"))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Peak Month"
#| icon: calendar-heart
#| color: success
list(value = peak_month)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Best Day of Week"
#| icon: star-fill
#| color: success
list(value = best_day)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Holiday Revenue Lift"
#| icon: gift-fill
#| color: "#84A98C"
list(value = paste0(round(holiday_lift, 1), "%"))
```

### Row {height="75%"}

```{r}
#| title: "Monthly Revenue Trend"

monthly_trend <- sales |>
  mutate(year_month = floor_date(date, "month")) |>
  group_by(year_month) |>
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(revenue), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(monthly_trend, aes(x = year_month, y = total_revenue)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2C5F2D", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(color = "#2C5F2D", size = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_dollar(scale = 1/1000, suffix = "K")) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "2 months") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Total Revenue") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
#| title: "Average Revenue by Product Category and Month"

cat_month <- sales |>
  group_by(category, month) |>
  summarise(avg_revenue = mean(revenue), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(cat_month, aes(x = month, y = category, fill = avg_revenue)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#EAF4EA", high = "#1B4332", labels = label_dollar()) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "Avg Revenue") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

## Column {width="35%"}

### Row {height="55%"}

```{r}
#| title: "Average Revenue by Day of Week"

ggplot(dow_revenue, aes(x = day_of_week, y = avg_revenue, fill = day_of_week)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rep(gold_green, length.out = 7)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_dollar()) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Avg Revenue per Transaction") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

### Row {height="45%"}

```{r}
#| title: "Holiday vs. Non-Holiday Revenue"

holiday_compare |>
  mutate(holiday = if_else(holiday, "Holiday", "Non-Holiday")) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(holiday = "Date Type", avg_revenue = "Avg Revenue") |>
  fmt_currency(columns = avg_revenue, decimals = 2) |>
  data_color(
    columns = avg_revenue,
    method = "numeric",
    palette = c("#EAF4EA", "#1B4332")
  ) |>
  tab_options(table.font.size = "0.85em")
```

# By Category

## Column {width="100%"}

### Row {height="60%"}

::: {.card title="Monthly Revenue Trend by Category"}

Each line below tracks total monthly revenue for one product category, letting you compare how seasonality plays out differently across Clothing, Electronics, Furniture, and Groceries.

```{r}
cat_trend <- sales |>
  mutate(year_month = floor_date(date, "month")) |>
  group_by(category, year_month) |>
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(revenue), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(cat_trend, aes(x = year_month, y = total_revenue, color = category)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = gold_green[1:4]) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_dollar(scale = 1/1000, suffix = "K")) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "3 months") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Total Revenue", color = "Category") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

:::

### Row {height="40%"}

```{r}
#| title: "Category Summary Statistics"

sales |>
  group_by(category) |>
  summarise(
    total_revenue   = sum(revenue),
    avg_units_sold  = mean(units_sold),
    avg_discount    = mean(discount_pct),
    holiday_share   = mean(holiday) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(total_revenue)) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    category       = "Category",
    total_revenue  = "Total Revenue",
    avg_units_sold = "Avg Units Sold",
    avg_discount   = "Avg Discount %",
    holiday_share  = "% Sold on Holidays"
  ) |>
  fmt_currency(columns = total_revenue, decimals = 0) |>
  fmt_number(columns = avg_units_sold, decimals = 1) |>
  fmt_number(columns = avg_discount, decimals = 1, pattern = "{x}%") |>
  fmt_number(columns = holiday_share, decimals = 1, pattern = "{x}%") |>
  data_color(
    columns = total_revenue,
    method = "numeric",
    palette = c("#EAF4EA", "#1B4332")
  ) |>
  tab_options(table.font.size = "0.9em")
```
